title: Natural laundry powder
subtitle: A washing soda and bicarbonate powder for everyday washing
excerpt: A two-ingredient dry laundry powder made from washing soda and bicarbonate of soda. Washing soda is the active cleaning agent, effective against grease and organic stains; bicarbonate softens the wash water and deodorises fabrics. Works in any machine temperature and is septic-tank safe.
sourceNotes: Soda crystals usage guidance, Dri-Pak Ltd (dri-pak.co.uk, May 2026). Sodium carbonate and sodium bicarbonate cleaning chemistry, UK Cleaning Products Industry Association notes.

content.text: A dry laundry powder. Washing soda (sodium carbonate) is the active cleaning agent: a strong alkali that breaks down grease and organic stains, softens hard water by precipitating calcium and magnesium ions, and raises the pH of the wash water to improve cleaning efficiency. Bicarbonate of soda (sodium bicarbonate) is milder; it softens wash water at a lower pH and deodorises fabrics. The two together outperform either alone on a wide range of household stains.

This powder contains no surfactant (soap or detergent). It performs well on grease, food, sweat, and body-oil stains. It is less effective on protein-based stains (blood, egg, milk) at high temperatures, where a cold pre-soak in the powder solution (15 minutes) before washing improves results. Not suitable for silk or wool: washing soda is too alkaline for protein fibres. Use a wool-safe detergent for those.

items.name: Washing soda
items.prepNote: washing soda crystals (sodium carbonate / soda crystals). 65% of the powder. The primary cleaning and water-softening agent. Available from supermarkets; look for Dri-Pak brand in the cleaning aisle.

items.name: Bicarbonate of soda
items.prepNote: fine bicarbonate of soda (baking soda). 35% of the powder. Deodorises and gently softens water. Moderates the alkalinity of the washing soda.

content.text: Weigh both powders into a large bowl or directly into a sealable jar and stir thoroughly with the silicone spatula to distribute evenly. Transfer to a sealed glass jar or tin. Label with the contents and batch date.

Add 1-2 tablespoons (15-30 g) directly into the drum of the washing machine, not the detergent drawer; the powder dissolves better on direct contact with clothes and water. Use the lower amount for a lightly loaded machine or soft water area; the higher amount for a heavily loaded machine or hard water area. Works at all temperatures from 30°C to 60°C. For whites and heavily soiled items, a 40°C or 60°C wash gives best results.

Safe for: cotton, linen, polyester, nylon, and most synthetic blends. Not suitable for: wool, silk, cashmere, and other protein fibres (washing soda at full strength will felt wool and degrade silk). For delicates, use a dedicated wool-safe liquid detergent.

2 years in a sealed, dry container. Both ingredients are hygroscopic and absorb atmospheric moisture if left open; this causes clumping but does not affect cleaning performance. Keep the lid on between washes. Do not store in a damp utility room without a sealed container.
